Why your FCP is fast but your INP is tanking, the truth about Hydration, and how modern Hybrid architectures fix it all.
If you have ever stared at a blank white screen waiting for a web app to load, you’ve experienced the front lines of the web rendering war.
Choosing between Server-Side Rendering (SSR), Client-Side Rendering (CSR), and Static Site Generation (SSG) is one of the most consequential architectural decisions a development team can make. But the modern web doesn’t stop there — we are now in the era of Hybrid Rendering.
To understand which strategy to choose, we have to look at Google’s Core Web Vitals, unravel a few massive industry misconceptions, and explore how new hybrid approaches are giving us the best of both worlds.
📏 The Metrics Lexicon: Core Web Vitals & Friends
Since 2020, Google has enforced Core Web Vitals (CWVs), a subset of metrics used as a ranking signal in search results to evaluate actual user experience.
The Official Core Web Vitals (Google’s scoreboard)

• LCP (Largest Contentful Paint): Loading Performance. The time it takes for the main, largest piece of content (like a…
